_comment: Fixed eval set for Ora. Keep it stable so result sets are comparable across runs/variants. Skewed toward contested, multi-dimensional, genuinely-uncertain questions so the rubric's `calibration` dimension (the one the Critic -> rebuttal -> synthesis seam most affects) can discriminate between variants. Add questions over time; don't edit existing ones in place (it breaks comparability).

questions.question: What are the systemic risks of AI in financial markets?
questions.why: Ora's own canonical example; contested, spans technical/economic/regulatory angles.

questions.question: How does mRNA vaccine technology work, and what are its main limitations?
questions.why: Technical depth plus real limitations/uncertainty — tests depth and calibration together.

questions.question: What caused the 2008 financial crisis?
questions.why: Historical, multi-causal, genuinely contested attribution — punishes overconfident single-cause answers.

questions.question: Is nuclear power a good solution for climate change?
questions.why: Value-laden; a good report must surface counter-arguments, not pick a side — directness vs calibration tension.

questions.question: What are the long-term cognitive effects of regular cannabis use?
questions.why: Science is genuinely unsettled; the strongest discriminator for the calibration dimension.

questions.question: How effective are GLP-1 drugs like tirzepatide for long-term weight management?
questions.why: Emerging evidence with real unknowns (durability, off-drug rebound); rewards calibrated reporting.
